title: jatropha peltata
intro: Jatropha peltata, also known as the cat's claw creeper or buffalo's horn, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Jatropha peltata include its ability to treat digestive issues, fever, and infections, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, Jatropha peltata is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the roots and leaves being the most commonly used parts of the plant.
